# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2023-01-11

# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe ISD Audit Committee Meeting
### January 11, 2023 — Regular Meeting (7:30 AM)

> **Note:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript of the meeting. They are not official minutes and may contain errors or omissions.

---

## Members & Attendees
- **Board President / Superintendent:** Dr. Noel (presided over initial portion)
- **Audit Committee Members:** Including Mrs. Chase (newly elected chair), Mr. Hubert, and other members (some new to the committee)
- **District Staff:** Darren Rice (Chief Financial Officer), Brian Hayden (Internal Auditor), Dr. Horton (Fine Arts), Marshall (Custodial/Maintenance Director)
- **External Auditor:** Sarah Roberts, Partner, Weaver

---

## Item 1: Citizens Participation
No members of the public signed up to speak.

---

## Item 2: Nomination and Approval of Audit Committee Chair

Dr. Noel explained the chair's role: presiding over meetings, working on agendas, and conducting the annual review of the internal auditor. As board president, Dr. Noel noted he is not eligible to serve as chair.

**Nomination:** Mrs. Chase was nominated (noted as "an auditor by trade" from a Fortune 100 company). She accepted the nomination.

**Vote:** Approved unanimously by show of hands. Mrs. Chase assumed the role of presiding officer for the remainder of the meeting.

---

## Item 3: Approval of Minutes from September 14, 2022 Audit and Investment Committee Meeting

**Motion:** To approve minutes as presented.
**Vote:** Approved unanimously.

---

## Item 4: Presentation of Annual Comprehensive Financial Report (ACFR) by Weaver

**Presenter:** Sarah Roberts, Partner, Weaver (external auditor for approximately 14 years)

### Introduction
- Darren Rice introduced the district's six-member finance team, noting no management comments in the report for approximately 14 years.
- The district consistently receives the GFOA (Government Finance Officers Association) Certificate of Achievement for Excellence in Financial Reporting.

### Audit Scope and Standards
- **Type of Audit:** Single audit (financial statement audit + federal awards audit)
- **Standards:** GAAS, Government Auditing Standards (Yellow Book), and Uniform Guidance (2 CFR 200)
- **Three Reports Issued:**
  1. Independent Auditor's Report (opinion on financial statements)
  2. Yellow Book Report (internal controls and compliance)
  3. Uniform Guidance Report (federal award compliance)

### Audit Results — All Clean
- **Financial Statements:** Unmodified (clean) opinion — highest level of assurance
- **Yellow Book Report:** Clean — no material weaknesses, no significant deficiencies, no material non-compliance
- **Federal Awards Report:** Unmodified (clean) opinion — no findings; approximately 50% coverage of ~$100 million in total federal expenditures

### Non-Audit Services (Independence Considerations)
1. A separate Weaver department has provided services to the internal audit function
2. Assistance with preparation of notes to the Schedule of Expenditures of Federal Awards (2 pages within the 160-page ACFR)
- Neither service impairs auditor independence

### Key Financial Highlights
- **Total net position:** Improved from a deficit of $12.7 million (prior year) to positive $30 million
- **Excluding pension/OPEB items:** Net position approaches $400 million (positive both years)
- **Major year-over-year changes:**
  - Issuance of Series 2022A bonds — approximately $340 million in new debt
  - Increased cash/investment balances and ongoing construction
  - Federal and state funding increased approximately $30 million
  - **New GASB 87 (Lease Standard):** Net impact less than $15,000 across five new line items

### General Fund Highlights
- Fund balance remarkably consistent year over year (increased ~$1 million)
- Unassigned fund balance covers approximately 95% of total fund balance
- Unassigned fund balance as a percent of expenditures: ~29-31% (approximately 4 months of operating coverage)

### Pension & OPEB Discussion (TRS)
- **Pension liability (net):** Decreased from $165 million to $86 million (nearly halved) due to outstanding TRS investment returns in fiscal year ending 8/31/2021 (~$40 billion in investment income vs. normal $10-15 billion)
- **Sensitivity analysis:** A 1% change in the discount rate could result in swings of up to $100 million for pension and significant swings for OPEB
- **Key context:** These are actuarial estimates reported on a one-year lag; the district has no control over TRS investment decisions
- **Deferred recognition:** Accounting standards smooth these swings — net pension effect changed only ~$7 million year-over-year (vs. $80 million raw swing)
- **TRS governance:** Board of Trustees is governor-appointed (some positions nominated by TRS membership); TRS manages ~$195 billion in assets
- Committee members noted the contradiction that school districts are restricted to conservative investments while TRS invests across all markets

### Upcoming Accounting Change
- **GASB 96 (Subscription-Based IT Arrangements):** Effective for fiscal year 2023; similar framework to lease standard; not expected to have a huge impact

---

## Item 5: Approval of Recommendation of ACFR to Full Board

**Motion:** To recommend the 2022 ACFR be presented to the Board of Trustees at the next regular board meeting.
**Vote:** Approved unanimously by show of hands.

*Note: Sarah Roberts will provide a briefer presentation to the full board the following week.*

---

## Item 6: Update on 2022-2023 Internal Audit Plan

**Presenter:** Brian Hayden, Internal Auditor (CPA, Certified Internal Auditor, Certified Fraud Examiner; 3.5 years with CISD)

### Internal Audit Department Overview
- Performs operational and financial audits (not curriculum or testing)
- Staff: One staff auditor dedicated to activity fund audits and PTO audits
- **Co-source arrangement with Weaver:** Provides supplemental resources, especially for IT audits, without the cost of additional full-time hires
- Audit plan is based on annual risk assessment (questionnaire sent to leadership ~April; plan approved by audit committee in May)
- **Significant shift over past 3-4 years:** Expanded from primarily campus activity fund audits to full-service internal audit covering major district systems (IT, transportation, police, payroll, etc.) while maintaining activity fund audits

### Completed Audits

#### 1. Activity Fund Audits
- Bozeman Intermediate and The Woodlands High School — completed

#### 2. Custodial/Maintenance Audit
**Two Findings (Opportunities for Improvement):**

**Finding 1 — Warehouse Inventory Accuracy:**
- Random physical count revealed a 5% initial error rate; expanded testing showed 12% error rate
- Warehouse processes ~13,000 items annually
- Root cause: Mostly packaging issues (e.g., partially empty boxes appearing full)
- Dollar impact: Approximately $1,000 in discrepancies
- **Recommendation:** Implement random monthly inventory counts
- **Management response:** Implementing automated monthly count alerts, similar to preventative maintenance system

**Finding 2 — Accident Report Documentation:**
- Reviewed all accident reports January 2021 through August 2022
- 37% error rate in report completeness (missing at-fault designations, drug test documentation, incomplete sections)
- ~75% of issues were incomplete form completion; ~25% were missing supporting documentation
- Only 19 accidents over the period across ~300 vehicles operating daily — a low accident rate
- **Recommendation:** Train supervisors on complete documentation; implement supervisory review
- **Management response:** Transitioning from paper to electronic forms with required fields and notification workflows
- Committee expressed concern about potential liability from incomplete records and employee welfare documentation

**Two Observations (Lower Risk):**

**Observation 1 — Attic Stock (Construction Leftover Materials):**
- Leftover construction materials (paint, tiles, doorknobs, etc.) stored at zero cost with no tracking controls
- **Recommendation:** Implement tracking controls for when items are removed from stock
- **Management response:** New warehouse will include inventory accounts for attic stock items

**Observation 2 — Duplicate Accident Reporting (Paper and Electronic):**
- Recommended choosing one format; electronic was selected

**Recognition:** Marshall (Maintenance Director) was noted as a state finalist from TASBO for operations management excellence.

#### 3. Band Instruments Audit (Follow-up — Two-Year Project)
- **Scope:** All junior highs, intermediates, and K-6 campuses (22 campuses visited)
- Physical inventory conducted with Dr. Horton (Fine Arts) at each campus
- **Results out of 2,752 instruments:**
  - 7 missing instruments (~$35,000 value)
  - 63 instruments added to Charms system (~$156,000 — already present but not recorded)
  - Various updates needed (wrong serial numbers, manufacturer, descriptions)
- **Context:**
  - ~5,600 instruments at capital outlay level across the district
  - ~40% teacher turnover in 5-8 grade campuses over last five years
  - Instrument purchases were delayed twice until inventory was accurate
  - Many "added" instruments were community donations or parent gifts
  - Teachers voluntarily worked nights and weekends to prepare for audits
- **Going forward:** Internal audit will step back; schools will maintain under established guidelines; random audits every other year by Dr. Horton
- Committee expressed appreciation for the urgency and thoroughness of this effort

### Remaining Audit Plan Items for 2022-2023

| Audit | Timing | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| **Child Nutrition** | In progress (work program development) | School-centric focus on cash controls and inventory; Weaver resources to assist with scale; state/federal auditors cover nutritional compliance |
| **IT Cybersecurity Plan Review** | February 2023 | Weaver-led; review of new cybersecurity plan against best-in-class standards |
| **IT General Controls Follow-up** | February 2023 | Follow-up on prior audit with 14 findings; separate Weaver team (same personnel as original audit) |
| **Caney Creek High School Remodel** | During year | Standard review of construction/remodel financials |
| **Police Department Follow-up** | Summer 2023 | Follow-up on 2021 audit |
| **Annual Attendance (PEIMS) Audit** | Summer 2023 | Annual verification that attendance is taken correctly (basis for state funding) |

---

## Next Meeting
**Confirmed:** Wednesday, May 10, 2023 at 7:30 AM

## Meeting Adjourned
Motion to adjourn was made and the meeting was concluded.
